attacking_style: Pure bunch sprinter who finishes the lead-out train rather than initiating attacks. Operates in mass sprint finales, not a rider who creates opportunities through positioning or late accelerations.
team_tactics: Relies on structured team lead-outs with Israel committing resources to deliver him into final 200m. Team investment suggests designated sprinter role, though conversion rate appears inconsistent.
terrain_preference: Flat, controlled bunch sprints where tactical errors by others create opportunities. Struggles when lead-out execution becomes critical to positioning against organized rival trains.
vulnerability: Converting team support into podium results - showed inability to finish when Israel executed their lead-out. Performance appears dependent on external factors like peloton tactical mistakes rather than overcoming organized opposition.
key_patterns: Inconsistent conversion of team lead-out support into top results (2 observations)
Performs better when race dynamics favor opportunism over pure speed (2 observations)
Requires structured team support but cannot always capitalize on investment (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: Mixed results with second place in 2021 Giro stage but sixth despite team support in 2020, suggesting plateaued performance level
